page: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and a shared CPU allocation. The memory subsystem provides 256.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is comprised of a 5760 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates competitive compute capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test recorded a score of 3091.16, placing it in the top tier (top 10%) of tested instances. Multi-core performance under stress-ng reached 99520.25, which is categorized as strong (top 25%). These metrics indicate robust processing capabilities for parallelized workloads, though the shared CPU allocation model represents a potential tradeoff for sustained, non-burstable compute demands.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local NVMe storage offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and data-heavy operations. The absence of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for hardware-accelerated machine learning tasks. However, the hardware profile is highly appropriate for large-scale databases, memory-cached applications, data warehousing, and enterprise-level web serving.
description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and a 5760 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. In benchmark testing, the server achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 3091.16, placing it in the top 10%, and a multi-core score of 99520.25, ranking in the top 25%. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained workloads, the high memory density and substantial NVMe storage capacity deliver excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments. This configuration is well-suited for large databases, memory caching, and enterprise applications.
